task.name: Create shared types for transaction analysis
task.verify: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` and confirm no type errors in the new file.

task.name: Create Zod validation schemas for transaction analysis
task.action:
Create `src/lib/validations/transaction-analysis.ts` with two schemas:
1. `transactionAnalysisQuerySchema` (server-side, for API route): - type: z.enum(["expense", "income"]) - startDate: z.string().optional() (validated as ISO date via .pipe(z.coerce.date()) or manual check in route) - endDate: z.string().optional() - categoryId: z.string().min(1).optional() - subcategoryId: z.string().min(1).optional() - tagIds: z.string().optional() (comma-separated string; the route will split and validate) - accountId: z.string().min(1).optional() - search: z.string().max(100).optional() - skip: z.coerce.number().int().min(0).default(0) - take: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).max(50).default(5) Export the schema and its inferred type.
2. `transactionAnalysisFormSchema` (client-side, for React Hook Form): - type: z.enum(["expense", "income"]) - startDate: z.date().optional().nullable() - endDate: z.date().optional().nullable() - categoryId: z.string().optional() - subcategoryId: z.string().optional() - tagIds: z.array(z.string()).optional() - accountId: z.string().optional() - search: z.string().max(100).optional() Export the schema and its inferred type `TransactionAnalysisFormValues`.
Follow the pattern in `src/lib/validations/expense-transactions.ts`.
task.verify: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` and confirm no type errors.

task.name: Create transaction analysis API route
task.action:
Create `src/app/api/reports/transaction-analysis/route.ts` with a GET handler.
Follow the auth pattern from `src/app/api/reports/expense-breakdown/route.ts`: - `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` - Import `auth` from `@/lib/auth`, `headers` from `next/headers`, `db` from `@/lib/prisma` - Check session, return 401 if missing - Extract and validate query params using `transactionAnalysisQuerySchema` - Return 400 on validation failure with descriptive error
Build a dynamic Prisma `where` clause: - Always include `userId` from session - For expenses: always include `isInstallment: false` - Conditionally add: date range (gte/lte with proper end-of-day for endDate), categoryId mapped to `expenseTypeId` or `incomeTypeId`, subcategoryId mapped to `expenseSubcategoryId`, accountId, name search with `contains` + `mode: 'insensitive'`, tags with `tags: { some: { id: { in: tagIdArray } } }`
Three queries in parallel via `Promise.all`: 1. Aggregate: `db.[model].aggregate({ where, _sum: { amount: true }, _count: true })` for totalAmount and transactionCount 2. Breakdown: `db.[model].groupBy` by the appropriate field: - No categoryId: group by expenseTypeId/incomeTypeId, then map IDs to names via a separate query for all user's types - categoryId set + type is expense + no subcategoryId: group by expenseSubcategoryId, map IDs to names. Null subcategory groups into "Uncategorized" - Otherwise (subcategoryId set, or income with categoryId): return empty breakdown Calculate percentages: `Math.round((itemAmount / totalAmount) * 10000) / 100` Sort by amount descending 3. Transactions: `db.[model].findMany({ where, skip, take: take + 1, orderBy: { date: 'desc' }, include: { account: { select: { name: true } }, [typeRelation]: { select: { name: true } }, ...(expense ? { expenseSubcategory: { select: { name: true } } } : {}) } })` - Slice to `take` items, set `hasMore = fetched.length > take` - Map to response shape with `amount` as positive number (`Math.abs(parseFloat(...))`)
Return JSON response matching `TransactionAnalysisResponse`.
Wrap in try/catch, log errors, return 500 on failure.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` to confirm the route compiles. Manual test: Start dev server, call `GET /api/reports/transaction-analysis?type=expense` in browser/curl (with valid session cookie). Confirm 200 response with correct shape.

task.name: Create useTransactionAnalysis hook
task.action:
Create `src/hooks/useTransactionAnalysis.ts`.
Import `useQuery` from `@tanstack/react-query`. Import `TransactionAnalysisParams` and `TransactionAnalysisResponse` from `@/types/transaction-analysis`.
Define a `fetchTransactionAnalysis` function that: - Accepts `TransactionAnalysisParams` - Builds `URLSearchParams` from the params, joining `tagIds` array with commas - Skips undefined/empty params - Fetches `GET /api/reports/transaction-analysis?${params}` - Throws on non-ok response - Returns typed `TransactionAnalysisResponse`
Export `useTransactionAnalysis(params: TransactionAnalysisParams)`: - `useQuery` with: - queryKey: `['transactionAnalysis', params]` - queryFn: `() => fetchTransactionAnalysis(params)` - enabled: false (manual trigger only) - staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000 - refetchOnWindowFocus: false - Return `{ data: data ?? null, isLoading, isFetching, error: error ? (error as Error).message : null, refetch }`
Follow the return pattern from `useExpenseBreakdown.ts`.
task.verify: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to confirm no type errors.

task.name: Create TransactionAnalyzer filter form UI
task.action:
Create `src/components/reports/TransactionAnalyzer.tsx` as a `"use client"` component.
This task focuses on the FILTER FORM portion only. The results display will be added in the next task.
Component structure: 1. Set up React Hook Form with `zodResolver(transactionAnalysisFormSchema)` and default values: `{ type: "expense", startDate: null, endDate: null, categoryId: "", subcategoryId: "", tagIds: [], accountId: "", search: "" }` 2. Use `useExpenseTypesQuery()`, `useIncomeTypesQuery()`, `useTagsQuery()`, `useAccountsQuery()` for filter dropdown data. 3. Use `useTransactionAnalysis` hook with params derived from form values. 4. Track `analysisParams` in state -- only updated when user clicks Analyze. 5. Track `accumulatedTransactions` in state for load-more pagination.
Render a Shadcn `Card` with `CardHeader` (title: "Transaction Analyzer", description: "Filter and analyze your transactions") and `CardContent`.
Inside `CardContent`, render the filter `Form`:
a. **Type toggle:** `ToggleGroup` with `type="single"`, `variant="outline"`, value from form, two items "Expense"/"Income". On change: update form value, reset categoryId and subcategoryId.
b. **Filter grid:** `div` with `grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-4`: - Start Date: FormField wrapping a Popover+Calendar date picker. Label "From". Display formatted date or "Start date" placeholder. - End Date: Same pattern. Label "To". - Category: FormField wrapping Select. Label "Category". Conditional options based on type toggle. Include "All categories" as first option with value "". When category changes, reset subcategoryId. - Subcategory: FormField wrapping Select. Label "Subcategory". **Only rendered** when type is "expense" AND categoryId is set and non-empty. Options from the selected expense type's `subcategories` array. Include "All subcategories" as first option with value "". - Tags: FormField wrapping Popover+Command combobox. Label "Tags". Inside the Command, render each tag as a CommandItem with a Checkbox. Selected tags shown as Badge pills below the popover trigger. Multi-select behavior via tagIds array in form. - Account: FormField wrapping Select. Label "Account". Include "All accounts" as first option. - Name Search: FormField wrapping Input with Search icon (Lucide). `md:col-span-2`. Label "Search by name". Placeholder "Transaction name...".
c. **Action buttons:** `div` with `flex justify-end gap-2`: - "Clear Filters" Button (ghost variant). Only visible when at least one non-type filter is active. onClick: reset form to defaults, clear results state, clear accumulatedTransactions. - "Analyze" Button (default variant). onClick: convert form values to `TransactionAnalysisParams`, set `analysisParams` state (which triggers refetch via useEffect or direct refetch call), reset accumulated transactions, set skip to 0. Disabled while isFetching. Show Spinner + "Analyzing..." when isFetching.
Do NOT render results yet -- just export the component with the filter form. The next task adds results.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Temporarily import TransactionAnalyzer in reports page (or just verify it compiles). Confirm filters render correctly on the dev server.

task.name: Add results display to TransactionAnalyzer
task.action:
Extend `src/components/reports/TransactionAnalyzer.tsx` to add the results panel below the filter form, inside the same `CardContent`.
The results panel is only shown when `data` from `useTransactionAnalysis` is non-null (i.e., after first successful query).
Sections (wrapped in `div className="space-y-6"`):
1. **Active filters display:** - `div` with `flex flex-wrap gap-2` - Render Badge pills for each active filter. Type badge is always shown, not removable. - Other badges have an X button (Lucide X icon, size 14). Clicking X removes that filter from the form, then re-triggers analysis. - Badge format: "Type: Expense", "From: Mar 1, 2026", "To: Mar 25, 2026", "Category: Food", "Subcategory: Restaurants", "Tag: Vacation" (one per tag), "Account: BPI Savings", "Search: coffee".
2. **Summary cards:** - `div` with `grid grid-cols-2 gap-4` - Two Cards (`p-4 text-center`): - "Total Amount": formatted as PHP currency using `Intl.NumberFormat('en-PH', { style: 'currency', currency: 'PHP' })`, `text-xl md:text-2xl font-bold` - "Transactions": the count number, same text sizing - Label above value: `text-xs md:text-sm text-muted-foreground`
3. **Breakdown section** (conditional: only shown when breakdown array is non-empty): - Heading: `text-sm font-semibold mb-3`. Text: "Breakdown by Category" (no categoryId) or "Breakdown by Subcategory" (categoryId set). - Each row: `div` with `space-y-1 mb-3`: - Top line: `flex justify-between` with name (left, `text-sm font-medium`) and amount + percentage (right, `text-sm text-muted-foreground`). Format: "PHP 1,234.56 (45.2%)" - Below: Shadcn `Progress` component with `value={item.percentage}`. Style the indicator color with inline style using HSL: `hsl((index * 360) / total, 65%, 60%)`. If Progress doesn't support indicator color via className, use a CSS variable override or a wrapper with a custom style.
4. **Transaction list** (conditional: only shown when transactions exist): - Heading: `text-sm font-semibold mb-3` "Matching Transactions" - Each transaction row: `div` with `flex items-center justify-between py-3 border-b last:border-b-0`: - Left: name (`text-sm font-medium`) + category info (`text-xs text-muted-foreground`) - Right: formatted amount (`text-sm font-medium`) + formatted date (`text-xs text-muted-foreground`) - "Load More" button at bottom when `hasMore` is true. Button `variant="outline" size="sm"`. On click: increment skip by 10 in the params, call refetch, append new transactions to `accumulatedTransactions` state. Show Spinner while loading.
5. **Empty state** (when transactionCount === 0): - Use `EmptyState` component with `variant="widget"`, icon `SearchX` from Lucide, title "No transactions found", description "Try adjusting your filters to find what you're looking for."
6. **Loading state** (when isFetching and no data yet): - Summary area: two Skeleton rectangles in grid - Breakdown: 3 Skeleton bars (h-8 each with gap) - Transactions: 3 Skeleton rows (h-12 each with gap)
7. **Error state:** - Show error message text + "Retry" button that calls refetch.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. On dev server: go to /reports, use the Transaction Analyzer. Click Analyze with default filters. Verify summary cards, breakdown, and transaction list all render. Test load more. Test empty state by searching for a gibberish name. Test the active filter badges and their removal.

task.name: Integrate TransactionAnalyzer into the reports page
task.action:
Modify `src/app/reports/page.tsx`:
1. Add import: `import TransactionAnalyzer from '@/components/reports/TransactionAnalyzer'` (or named import if using named export). 2. Inside the `div.space-y-6` container, after `<AnnualSummaryTable />`, add: `<TransactionAnalyzer />`
The component is self-contained, so no props are needed.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. On dev server: navigate to /reports. Scroll past Annual Summary. The Transaction Analyzer card should be visible and functional. Verify the layout matches the rest of the page (max-w-5xl constraint, consistent spacing).

task.name: Run full lint and build check
task.action: Run `npm run lint` and fix any errors. Run `npm run build` and ensure zero errors. Run through the verification plan in the spec (section 6) for manual testing.
task.verify: Both commands pass with zero errors. All manual verification checks from spec section 6.2 pass.

task.name: Generate verification document
task.action: Create `docs/transaction-analyzer-verification.md` documenting: - All files created and modified - Lint and build results - Manual test results for each verification item from spec section 6.2 - Any deviations from the spec and rationale
task.verify: The verification document exists and covers all items.

phase.name: 7a: CSS Foundation and Animated Field Reveal

task.name: Add .field-reveal CSS class and reduced-motion support
task.verify: Run `npm run build`. The CSS compiles without errors. On dev server, inspect the `.field-reveal` class in browser DevTools — confirm the `grid-template-rows` transition property exists.

task.name: Apply animated reveal to subcategory field
task.action:
In `src/components/reports/TransactionAnalyzer.tsx`: Replace the conditional `{showSubcategory && (...)}` render of the Subcategory FormField with an always-rendered wrapper: ```tsx {/* Subcategory — animated reveal */}
``` The Subcategory FormField content stays exactly the same — only the outer wrapper changes from conditional render to animated reveal. Important: The `showSubcategory` boolean already exists. Just use it as the `data-visible` attribute value (convert to string if needed: `data-visible={showSubcategory || undefined}` or use `data-visible={showSubcategory ? "true" : undefined}`).
task.verify: On dev server: Select an expense category that has subcategories. The subcategory field should smoothly animate into view (height slides open over 200ms). Deselect or pick "All categories" — field slides closed. Test with DevTools → Rendering → "prefers-reduced-motion: reduce" → field should appear/disappear instantly.

phase.name: 7b: Mobile Form Density and Collapsible Filters

task.name: Reduce mobile form spacing
task.action:
In `src/components/reports/TransactionAnalyzer.tsx`:
1. Change the form element's `className` from `space-y-4` to `space-y-3 md:space-y-4`. 2. Change the filter grid's `gap-4` to `gap-3 md:gap-4`. 3. Change the `CardContent` `space-y-6` to `space-y-4 md:space-y-6`.
task.verify: On dev server at mobile viewport (375px): Confirm tighter spacing between form fields. Labels and inputs remain readable. Touch targets are still ≥ 24px tall. On desktop viewport: Spacing unchanged (gap-4, space-y-4, space-y-6).

task.name: Add collapsible "More Filters" section for mobile
task.action:
In `src/components/reports/TransactionAnalyzer.tsx`: 1. Add a `moreFiltersOpen` state: `const [moreFiltersOpen, setMoreFiltersOpen] = useState(false)`. 2. Compute active Tier 2 filter count: ```tsx const tier2ActiveCount = [ selectedTagIds.length > 0, !!form.watch("accountId"), !!form.watch("search"), ].filter(Boolean).length; ``` 3. Split the filter grid into two sections: **Tier 1 (always visible):** Date range (From, To), Category, Subcategory (with field-reveal). **Tier 2 (collapsible on mobile):** Tags, Account, Search by name. Between them, add a trigger button (mobile only): ```tsx ``` Note: Import `ChevronUp` from lucide-react (ChevronDown is likely already imported as `ChevronDownIcon`). 4. Wrap Tier 2 fields in the `.field-reveal` pattern for mobile, always visible on desktop: ```tsx
{/* Tags field */} {/* Account field */} {/* Search field (md:col-span-2) */}
``` The `md:!grid-rows-[1fr]` ensures desktop always shows the fields regardless of `data-visible`. 5. The Tier 1 fields remain in their own `grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-3 md:gap-4` div above the trigger.
task.verify: On mobile viewport (375px): - Tier 1 filters (dates, category) visible by default. - "More Filters" trigger visible. Clicking it smoothly reveals Tags, Account, Search. - Active count badge shows when Tier 2 filters have values. On desktop viewport: - "More Filters" trigger is hidden (`md:hidden`). - All fields visible in the 2-column grid as before.

phase.name: 7c: Results Summary, Cards, and Visual Breaks

task.name: Add summary sentence and replace count card with average
task.action:
In `src/components/reports/TransactionAnalyzer.tsx`: 1. **Summary sentence:** Add a new element between `ActiveFilters` and the summary cards grid. The sentence structure is: `You [spent/earned] [amount] across [count] [on [category [> subcategory]]] [in [account]] [date clause]` Each segment is independently conditional — omitted filters produce no text: - Category uses "on": `on Food & Dining` - Subcategory appends with " > ": `on Food & Dining > Restaurants` - Account uses "in": `in BPI Savings` - Both dates: `from Mar 1, 2026 to Mar 25, 2026` - Only start date: `since Mar 1, 2026` - Only end date: `up to Mar 25, 2026` Tags and name search are NOT included in the sentence (visible in filter pills above). To resolve category/subcategory/account names from IDs in `analysisParams`, look up the name from the `categories`, `subcategories`, and `accounts` arrays already available in the component. Dynamic values use `` for emphasis. Outer `

` uses `text-sm md:text-base text-muted-foreground`. 2. **Replace count card with average:** Change the second summary card from: - Label: "Transactions" → "Avg. per Transaction" - Value: `data.transactionCount` → `formatCurrency(data.totalAmount / data.transactionCount)` - Handle edge case: if `transactionCount === 0`, show `formatCurrency(0)`.
task.verify: On dev server: Run an analysis. - Summary sentence appears between filter pills and cards: "You spent PHP X across N transactions". - With category selected, sentence includes "on [Category Name]". - With category + subcategory, sentence shows "on [Category] > [Subcategory]". - With account selected, sentence includes "in [Account Name]". - With dates selected, sentence includes "from [date] to [date]". - With only one date, shows "since [date]" or "up to [date]". - With no optional filters, sentence is just "You spent PHP X across N transactions". - Second card shows "Avg. per Transaction" with correct value (total / count).

task.name: Add visual section breaks and results container
task.action:
In `src/components/reports/TransactionAnalyzer.tsx`:
1. **Separator between form and results:** Import `Separator` from `@/components/ui/separator`. If the component doesn't exist yet, check with `ls src/components/ui/separator*`. If not present, install via `npx shadcn@latest add separator`.
Add `<Separator className="my-4 md:my-6" />` between the `</Form>` closing tag and the results panel `{hasAnalyzed && data && (...)}`— inside the results conditional, as the first element.
2. **Results background container:** Wrap the entire results content (everything inside `{hasAnalyzed && data && (...)}`) in: ```tsx <div className="rounded-lg bg-muted/20 p-3 md:p-4 space-y-4 md:space-y-6"> ```
3. **Sub-section heading accents:** Update "Breakdown by ..." and "Matching Transactions" headings to include a left border: ```tsx <h3 className="text-sm font-semibold mb-3 pl-3 border-l-2 border-primary/40"> ```
task.verify: On dev server: Run an analysis. - A horizontal separator line appears between the form and results. - Results section has a subtle muted background with rounded corners. - "Breakdown by Category" and "Matching Transactions" headings have a teal left border accent. - Visual hierarchy is clear: form (input) → separator → results (output).

task.name: Add interaction states to filter pills
task.action:
In the `ActiveFilters` helper component inside `src/components/reports/TransactionAnalyzer.tsx`:
1. Update the Badge wrapper for removable filters to include hover state: ```tsx <Badge variant="secondary" className="text-xs hover:bg-secondary/80 transition-colors duration-150"> ```
2. Update the dismiss button (the X icon button) inside each removable badge: ```tsx <button type="button" className="ml-1 rounded-full p-0.5 hover:bg-foreground/10 focus-visible:ring-1 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:outline-none transition-colors duration-150 active:scale-95 cursor-pointer" onClick={() => onRemove(filterKey)} > <X className="h-3 w-3" /> </button> ```
3. Ensure the type badge (non-removable) does NOT have hover:bg change or a dismiss button.
task.verify: On dev server: Run an analysis with multiple filters active. - Hovering a removable pill shows subtle background change. - Hovering the X button shows a circular highlight. - Clicking X removes the filter with tactile press feedback. - Tabbing through pills shows focus ring on X buttons. - The type pill ("Expense"/"Income") has no hover effect or X button.

task.name: Improve Load More with remaining count and skeleton rows
task.action:
In the transaction list section of `src/components/reports/TransactionAnalyzer.tsx`: 1. **Update button text** to show remaining count: ```tsx const remaining = data.transactionCount - accumulatedTransactions.length; ``` Button label when not loading: `Load More (${remaining} remaining)`. 2. **Add skeleton rows while loading:** When `isLoadingMore` is true, render 2-3 skeleton rows below the transaction list (above the Load More button): ```tsx {isLoadingMore && (
{[...Array(Math.min(remaining, 3))].map((_, i) => ( ))}
)} ``` 3. Button remains visible and disabled during loading (already the case), but now the spinner is supplemented by the skeletons above.
task.verify: On dev server: Run an analysis with enough results to trigger pagination. - Load More button shows "Load More (X remaining)". - Clicking Load More: button disables, 2-3 skeleton rows appear below existing transactions, new transactions append when loaded. - Existing transactions do NOT re-render or flash.

task.name: Lint, build, and comprehensive UI verification
task.action:
Run `npm run lint` and fix any errors. Run `npm run build` and ensure zero errors.
Manual verification checklist: 1. Subcategory field animates smoothly on show/hide (200ms ease-out). 2. Mobile (375px): tighter spacing, "More Filters" collapsible works, Tier 2 shows active count. 3. Desktop: all fields always visible, "More Filters" trigger hidden. 4. Summary sentence reads naturally for expense/income, with/without dates. 5. Second summary card shows "Avg. per Transaction" with correct math. 6. Filter pills have hover states, X button has hover/focus/active states. 7. Load More shows remaining count and skeleton rows while loading. 8. Separator visible between form and results. 9. Results wrapped in muted background container. 10. Breakdown and Transaction headings have left border accent. 11. prefers-reduced-motion disables field-reveal animations.
task.verify: Lint and build pass with zero errors. All 11 manual checks pass.
